{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"FAQPage","mainEntity":[{"@type":"Question","name":"How should schools use a head lice policy template?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Use the policy template as a governance baseline, then adapt communication timings and responsibilities to your setting while keeping language non-diagnostic."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"What should a lice letter to parents template include?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"It should include calm exposure context, practical home-check steps, and clear escalation timing, without naming pupils or making diagnostic claims."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"When should a school lice outbreak protocol be escalated?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Escalate when repeated reports continue across households, uncertainty remains after checks, or welfare concerns increase in a short period."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"Can this support a head lice policy for schools UK and outside the UK?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Yes. The framework is built for UK policy language and can be adapted for international settings by mapping local governance and safeguarding requirements."}}]}
